self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can improve whole-product truth quality by letting the canonical report inspect the historical Runtime registry-entry family directly.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP Architecture case materializes legacy Runtime registry-entry compatibility inside the shared state resolver.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before implementation, direct report focus on the historical `runtime/registry/*-registry-entry.md` family still threw unsupported-path errors.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Historical Runtime registry-entry inspection becomes explicit, bounded, and reportable without inventing new Runtime v0 advancement.

decision.rationale: The shared truth anchor should resolve historical Runtime registry entries as read-only Runtime history.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
